title: carex pisiformis var. duvaliana
intro: Carex pisiformis var. duvaliana, also known as Duval's Sedge, is a perennial plant species belonging to the Carex genus, typically found in wetlands and grasslands. It has been traditionally used in herbal medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, derived from its leaves and roots. The health benefits of Duval's Sedge include its ability to treat conditions such as skin irritations, wounds, and infections, as well as its potential to reduce inflammation and promote healing. Duval's Sedge is often used in herbal preparations such as infusions, salves, and tinctures, which are made by steeping the plant's leaves and roots in a solvent like water or alcohol.
